name: Cuenca
tagline: Colonial Charm and Eternal Spring — Retire on USD in the Andes
description: Cuenca is the crown jewel of FIRE retirement in Latin America. This UNESCO World Heritage city in the Ecuadorian Andes offers eternal spring weather, a thriving and welcoming expat community, world-class healthcare at rock-bottom prices, and the enormous advantage of using the US dollar as its currency — eliminating exchange rate risk entirely.

quickFacts.visa: Retirement Visa (Jubilado; requires $1,400/mo pension income). Investor and Professional visas also available. Path to citizenship after 3 years.
quickFacts.safetyRating: Safe — one of Ecuador's safest cities, standard precautions in markets and at night
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Mariscal Lamar Airport (CUE) — 10 min from city center; connect through Quito or Guayaquil for international flights
quickFacts.climate: Subtropical highland with eternal spring — 55-75°F year-round with a mild rainy season (Jan-May)

housing.description: Furnished 2-bedroom apartment in El Centro or near the Tomebamba River with colonial charm

dining.description: Almuerzos (set lunches) for $2.50-3.50, local restaurants, weekend market food, café culture

healthcare.description: IESS public insurance enrollment ($80/mo) plus cash-pay visits to excellent private clinics like Hospital Monte Sinaí

transportation.description: City buses at $0.30/ride, taxis across town for $2-3, walkable historic center

entertainment.description: Cajas National Park hikes, free cathedral visits, expat meetups, local concert scene, river walks

1M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom apartment in El Centro or along the Tomebamba River provides a comfortable base in a walkable colonial city. Daily almuerzos cost a few dollars, buses are cheap, and IESS public insurance covers basic healthcare. The expat community is large and welcoming, weekends in Cajas National Park are free, and the US dollar currency eliminates exchange rate risk. This budget leaves meaningful margin for savings.

housing.description: Spacious 3-bedroom apartment or small house in Yanuncay or El Vergel with garden and mountain views

dining.description: Mix of local almuerzos, international restaurants, Italian and sushi spots, regular café brunch

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance with Hospital Santa Inés access, dental work, annual checkups

transportation.description: Frequent taxi and rideshare use, weekend car rental for trips to Ingapirca or Guayaquil coast

entertainment.description: Gym membership, Spanish classes, weekend trips to coastal beaches, Cuenca arts scene, cooking classes

2M.lifestyle: A spacious house in Yanuncay or El Vergel with a garden and mountain views gives you a comfortable suburban base. You eat at international and local restaurants freely, carry comprehensive private insurance, and rent a car for weekend trips to the coast or Inca ruins at Ingapirca. Spanish classes help you integrate, and this budget provides significant margin in a city where costs are genuinely low.

housing.description: Luxury 4-bedroom house in Challuabamba or a renovated colonial home in El Centro with courtyard and modern finishes

dining.description: Fine dining at Cuenca's best restaurants weekly, private cooking classes, curated wine dinners

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance, top specialists, annual executive health screening, dental implants at a fraction of US cost

transportation.description: Owned or leased SUV for mountain excursions, regular taxi use, domestic flights to Quito and the Galápagos

entertainment.description: Country club membership, guided Galápagos trips, artisan workshop sponsorship, weekend hacienda stays

3M.lifestyle: A large house in Challuabamba or a renovated colonial home in El Centro with a courtyard puts you at the top of Cuenca's housing market. You dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, own an SUV for mountain excursions, and take domestic flights to the coast or Quito. Annual Galápagos trips are comfortably in budget. This is a genuinely affluent life in a city where $10,000 per month goes very far.

housing.description: Stunning hacienda-style estate in the Cuenca countryside or the finest restored colonial mansion in El Centro with rooftop terrace

dining.description: Private chef preparing Ecuadorian and international cuisine, exclusive hosted dinners, imported ingredients

healthcare.description: Top-tier private insurance with international coverage, fly-to-Miami option for major procedures, personal physician

transportation.description: Luxury SUV with part-time driver, domestic flights, private transfers to Guayaquil airport

entertainment.description: Annual Galápagos yacht charter, private cloud forest excursions, art collecting, cultural festival VIP access

domesticHelp.description: Full-time live-in housekeeper/cook, part-time gardener, weekly laundry service

luxuryExtras.description: Galápagos property rental, bespoke Panama hat commissions in nearby towns, charitable projects supporting local artisans

5M.lifestyle: A hacienda-style estate in the countryside or a top-end colonial mansion in El Centro provides generous space with Andean views. A housekeeper and cook manage the household, a part-time driver handles transportation, and you eat at the city's best restaurants without hesitation. Galápagos trips, cloud forest retreats, and business class flights for international travel are all comfortably covered. This budget far exceeds Cuenca's cost ceiling.

housing.description: Grand estate with multiple structures, gardens, and panoramic Andean views, plus a beach property on the Ecuadorian coast

dining.description: Private chef team, rare ingredient sourcing, legendary dinner parties, wine cellar curation with South American and European bottles

healthcare.description: Global concierge medicine, dedicated medical team, annual US or European health retreats, longevity programs

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle collection, full-time chauffeur, private flights to Quito and Guayaquil, international first-class travel

entertainment.description: Philanthropic foundations supporting Ecuadorian arts and education, private Galápagos expeditions, international travel, art patronage

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: chef, two housekeepers, gardener, driver, groundskeeper, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Coastal vacation home management, cacao plantation investment, Galápagos conservation sponsorship, bespoke cultural tours of Ecuador

10M.lifestyle: A grand estate with Andean views plus a coastal property on the Pacific gives you two distinct bases. A cook, housekeepers, and groundskeeping staff maintain both properties. You fly first class internationally, take private Galápagos expeditions, and dine without any budget constraints. This budget is well beyond what Cuenca can absorb in spending — substantial margin flows to savings, travel, or philanthropic involvement in Ecuador's arts and conservation.
